prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_svr', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and high seasonality variance (1.18), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA leads in seasonality correlation and FT_svr excels at trend tracking. The combination of strong early/late horizon specialists with tier1 models supports dba_combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths.

You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "rf", "FT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "catboost", "ARIMA", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.0441,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and high seasonality variance (1.18), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA leads in seasonality correlation and FT_svr excels at trend tracking. The combination of strong early/late horizon specialists with tier1 models supports dba_combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim0.8, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Universe leaderboards show robust_median as strong but current set already has diverse types. No redundancy (different methods). Score margin top2=0.0049 is acceptable.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.04) and high seasonality variance (1.18), indicating models capture patterns very differently. THETA dominates overall RMSE while ARIMA leads in seasonality correlation and FT_svr excels at trend tracking. The combination of strong early/late horizon specialists with tier1 models supports dba_combination as the optimal approach to leverage diverse model strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.38 (≥0.25) signals strong disagreement requiring robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights. RMSE spread ratio=1.04 shows significant dispersion between best (THETA=976.27) and worst (ONLY_FT_svr=2069.12) models, favoring top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and non-linear divergence. With n_windows=3, shrinkage=0.35 provides stability against overfitting.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 5 candidates with good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim0.8, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Universe leaderboards show robust_median as strong but current set already has diverse types. No redundancy (different methods). Score margin top2=0.0049 is acceptable.
